NAME: TITAN
FULL NAME: TITAN — Transformer-based pathology Image and Text Alignment Network
TITLE: A multimodal whole-slide foundation model for pathology.
DESCRIPTION: TITAN (Transformer-based pathology Image and Text Alignment Network) is a multimodal whole-slide foundation model from Mahmood Lab (Harvard/BWH). Pretrained on 335,645 WSIs via visual self-supervised learning and vision-language alignment with 423K synthetic captions from PathChat + 183K pathology reports. Without any fine-tuning, TITAN produces general-purpose slide representations for zero-shot classification, rare cancer retrieval, cross-modal retrieval, and pathology report generation. Outperforms both ROI and slide foundation models across diverse clinical tasks.
MANUAL_CITATION: Ding T, Wagner SJ, Song AH, et al. (2025) A multimodal whole-slide foundation model for pathology. Nat Med. 31:3749-3761. doi:10.1038/s41591-025-03982-3. PMID 41193692
CITATION: Ding T, Wagner SJ, Song AH, Chen RJ, Lu MY, Zhang A, Vaidya AJ, Jaume G, Shaban M, Kim A, Williamson DFK, Oldenburg L, Chen B, Alajaji A, Noor G, Sang Y, Peng T, Le LP, Mahmood F. (2025) A multimodal whole-slide foundation model for pathology. Nature Medicine, 31:3749-3761. doi:10.1038/s41591-025-03982-3. PMID 41193692
Authors: Ding T,Wagner SJ,Song AH,Chen RJ,Lu MY,Zhang A,Vaidya AJ,Jaume G,Shaban M,Kim A,Williamson DFK,Oldenburg L,Chen B,Alajaji A,Noor G,Sang Y,Peng T,Le LP,Mahmood F
ABSTRACT: The field of computational pathology has been transformed with recent advances in foundation models that encode histopathology region-of-interests into versatile feature representations. However, translating these advancements to address complex clinical challenges at the patient and slide level remains constrained by limited clinical data. We propose TITAN, a multimodal whole-slide foundation model pretrained using 335,645 whole-slide images via visual self-supervised learning and vision-language alignment with pathology reports and 423,122 synthetic captions. Without any fine-tuning, TITAN can extract general-purpose slide representations and generate pathology reports that generalize to resource-limited clinical scenarios such as rare disease retrieval and cancer prognosis.
